issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, such as box-shadow or border. This removes focus rings for all interactive elements when focused via keyboard, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes outline for non-keyboard focus events. Since :focus-visible also lacks a visible indicator, focus is not visible in any state, degrading accessibility for keyboard and assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; } // Only acceptable if :focus-visible has a visible indicator; ensure Issue 1 is fixed first.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

After `render()` finishes, build a short summary (e.g., “Filters applied: Category = Design, Tags = beta”) and call `liveRegion.announce(summary);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set `aria‑expanded` on page load (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s initial state is never given an `aria-expanded` value, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their state from the start.
issuesFound.fix:
After caching `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle missing `aria‑controls` on init (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: The switch controls a region of price information but does not reference it with `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship needed for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the price container has an `id` (e.g., `id="price-panel"`), then in `init` add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', 'price-panel');`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons do not update `aria‑pressed` on state change (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter tab is activated the script only toggles CSS classes; it never updates the required `aria-pressed` attribute, leaving the state invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_onTabClick` after changing `_productFilterState.activeCategory`, call `_syncTabAriaStates();` which sets `aria-pressed` appropriately (as described in Issue 4).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (site search suggestions)
issuesFound.explanation: The `siteSearch._open()` and `siteSearch._close()` functions show/hide the autocomplete list but never set `aria-expanded` on the search input. Screen readers cannot know whether the suggestion panel is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` toggle visibility of dropdown panels without updating `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Users of assistive tech cannot perceive the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` expand/collapse FAQ items but never set `aria-expanded` on the question button. The accordion’s state is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (mobile navigation trigger)
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) toggles the mobile menu but does not expose its pressed state via `aria-pressed`. Users cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.toggle()` (or after `open/close`) set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open))`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not removed from accessibility tree (mobile navigation drawer)
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` hides the drawer with CSS classes but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. The hidden drawer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not removed from accessibility tree (dropdown panels)
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._closeInstance()` hides the panel via CSS but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, leaving it focusable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` when closing and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not removed from accessibility tree (FAQ panels)
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.close()` hides the answer panel with `hidden` attribute but never sets `aria-hidden="true"`. The panel stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')` on close and remove it on open.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not removed from accessibility tree (search suggestion list)
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` adds `hidden` to the listbox but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. The list remains perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `hide()` add `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `show()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised at page load (mobile navigation trigger)
issuesFound.explanation: The nav‑toggle button lacks an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: On script init, set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised at page load (dropdown triggers)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: During `dropdownMenu.register()` set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised at page load (search input)
issuesFound.explanation: The search input lacks an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the live‑region and listbox, call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised at page load (FAQ triggers)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons lack an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when the item is initially closed.

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (mobile navigation open/close)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not provide an audible announcement, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `open()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened')`; after `close()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (dropdown menu open/close)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown does not trigger a live‑region announcement, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: User action not announced via live region (search suggestions panel)
issuesFound.explanation: Showing or hiding the autocomplete list does not generate a live‑region announcement, making the change invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After `_open()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchResultsFound || 'Search suggestions shown')`; after `_close()` call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchClearing || 'Search suggestions hidden')`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region (product filter count badge)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the product count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is updated but no live‑region is used to announce the new count, so users cannot know how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`)` or update a hidden live region with the count.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions manipulate panel visibility by adding or removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance: After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line _closeInstance: After `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the code updates the visual state and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistency where the expanded state is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox opens and closes dynamically based on user interaction, but the aria-expanded attribute on the input element is never updated to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is visible. This violates the ARIA combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
